issue.content:
name: 🏗️ Architecture proposal
description: Propose and validate one change to the LLM architecture.
title: "[ARCH-PROP] "

body:
  - type: markdown
    attributes:
      value: |
        # Architecture proposal

        Use this form to propose one architecture change. Keep the proposal focused and submit unrelated changes separately.

  # - type: dropdown
  #   id: proposal_type
  #   attributes:
  #     label: Proposal type
  #     options:
  #       - Root architecture
  #       - Modification to an existing architecture
  #   validations:
  #     required: true

  - type: input
    id: proposal_name
    attributes:
      label: Architecture Name
      description: Give the proposed base architecture or modification a short, descriptive name.
      placeholder: "Example: OLMo3"
    validations:
      required: true

  - type: input
    id: base_architecture_issue
    attributes:
      label: Parent issue
      description: For a modification, link the issue that tracks the base architecture. Leave blank for a root architecture.
      placeholder: "Example: #123"
    validations:
      required: false

  - type: textarea
    id: related_work
    attributes:
      label: Related work
      description: Optionally link related issues, pull requests, papers, experiments, or implementations.
      placeholder: |
        - #123
        - [Relevant paper](https://...)
    validations:
      required: false

  - type: textarea
    id: problem_and_hypothesis
    attributes:
      label: Motivations
      description: Describe the current limitation or opportunity, the measurable outcome you expect, and any supporting rationale or evidence.
      placeholder: Explain the problem and the expected outcome.
    validations:
      required: true

  - type: textarea
    id: proposed_change
    attributes:
      label: Proposed Architecture
      description: Describe what changes from the base architecture, what remains unchanged, and the expected impact on parameters, compute, memory, or latency if known.
      placeholder: Explain the proposed architecture change.
    validations:
      required: true

  - type: textarea
    id: validation_plan
    attributes:
      label: Experiments Plan
      description: Identify the baseline, evaluation method and metrics, measurable success criteria, and any notable compute or data requirements.
      placeholder: Explain how the proposal will be evaluated.
    validations:
      required: true

  - type: markdown
    attributes:
      value: |
        ---

        Repository maintainers record the decision using a `review:*` label and a structured reviewer comment. Proposal authors must not approve their own proposals.

pull_request.content:
## Title

## Basic information

- **Architecture Name:** 
- **Proposal Issue:**
- **Official Model:** 

## W&B Links
> W&B links are required for every training or validation PR.
> The links must provide reviewers with direct access to the
> complete training history

- **W&B Projects Link:**
- **Training Run Link:**
- **Benchmark Run Link:**

## Implementation Summary

## Experiments Summary

### Experiments Outcome

- [x] Positive
- [ ] Negative
- [ ] Neutral
- [ ] Inconclusive

### Reproduction Status

- [x] Basic architecture reproduction validated
- [ ] Official checkpoint equivalence validated
- [ ] Full training recipe reproduction validated

## Conclusion


## Merge Checklist

- [x] The linked proposal is approved.
- [x] The implementation matches the official architecture.
- [x] The upstream source and immutable revision are recorded.
- [x] Forward, backward, checkpoint, and training validation pass.
- [x] Training and evaluation configurations are committed.
- [x] Public W&B logs and benchmark results are linked.
- [x] Tests, documentation, and CI pass.
- [x] Required reviewers approve the PR.

issues.title: [ARCH-PROP] Olmo3
issues.author: scv11

related_work.text:
Papers or technical reports: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2501.00656
Relevant implementations: https://github.com/allenai/olmo

issues.motivations:
The repository does not currently have an immutable and reproducible OLMo 3 reference architecture.
A local implementation based on an official OLMo 3 configuration can provide a reliable root node for future architecture experiments.
issues.proposed_architecture:
Reproduce the selected official OLMo 3 Base configuration as a root architecture in this repository.
Parameter count, compute, memory usage, and latency should remain consistent with the selected official OLMo 3 model variant.
issues.experiments_plan:
Baseline
The selected official OLMo 3 configuration and upstream implementation.

Success criteria
Benchmark degradation <1%
Decode throughput achieve 1000 tokens/s

Compute or data requirements, if known
8xH200, 300B tokens

issues.title: [ARCH-PROP] Depth-Attention & SiameseNorm Support
issues.author: Feanus
issues.motivations:
Current OLMo-3 uses a standard Transformer architecture with conventional attention and normalization designs. While this architecture provides strong general-purpose performance, there are opportunities to improve depth-wise information propagation and training stability.

Expected measurable outcomes
- Lower validation perplexity compared with the OLMo-3 baseline.
- Improved downstream benchmark performance.
issues.proposed_architecture:
Replace the standard attention/normalization components in OLMo-3 with:

- **Depth-Attention:** adds attention over intermediate layer representations to improve depth-wise information exchange.
- **SiameseNorm:** replaces the existing normalization design with shared normalization across paired representations.

The rest of the architecture remains unchanged, including tokenizer, Transformer blocks, training objective, and data pipeline.
issues.experiments_plan:
**Baseline:**
- Original OLMo-3 architecture with identical training settings.

**Success criteria:**
- Achieve measurable improvement in validation perplexity and/or downstream accuracy.
- Demonstrate benefits that justify additional compute or memory overhead.

**Requirements:**
- Same dataset, tokenizer, and training pipeline as the baseline.
- Controlled ablation studies for Depth-Attention and SiameseNorm.

issues.title: [ARCH-PROP] MLA & MorphNorm Support
issues.author: Feanus
issues.motivations:
Current OLMo-3 uses standard multi-head attention (MHA), which has high KV cache memory cost and limits inference efficiency at long context lengths. Meanwhile, existing normalization approaches may not fully address representation stability in large-scale Transformer training.

This proposal introduces:
- **Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA):** compresses KV representations into a latent space, reducing KV cache size and improving long-context inference efficiency while preserving attention capacity.
- **MorphNorm:** improves normalization behavior by adapting representation scaling, aiming to improve training stability and model quality.
issues.proposed_architecture:
Replace the standard attention and normalization components in OLMo-3 with:

- **MLA:** replace MHA with latent KV compression and latent attention computation to reduce KV cache size.
- **MorphNorm:** replace the existing normalization layer with MorphNorm for improved representation scaling.

The rest of the architecture remains unchanged, including tokenizer, Transformer block structure, training objective, and data pipeline.

Expected impact:
- Reduced KV cache memory footprint during inference.
- Potentially improved long-context inference throughput.
- Slight changes in attention compute due to latent projection and reconstruction.
- Minimal additional overhead from MorphNorm.
- Parameter and latency impact will be measured through benchmarking.
issues.experiments_plan:
**Baseline:**
- Original OLMo-3 architecture with standard MHA and normalization.

**Success criteria:**
- Match or improve baseline language modeling performance.
- Achieve meaningful KV cache reduction and inference efficiency gains.
- Ensure additional compute overhead remains acceptable.

**Requirements:**
- Same dataset, tokenizer, and training pipeline.
- Ablation studies for MLA and MorphNorm individually.

pull_requests.title: OLMo-3 1B Base Architecture
pull_requests.author: scv11
pull_requests.implementation_summary:
This PR adds OLMo2-1B as a reproducible root architecture in this
repository.

It includes the model configuration, decoder blocks, training and evaluation
configurations, checkpoint support, and validation required to use OLMo2-1B as a baseline for future architecture experiments.

Reference to [olmo2-0425-1b](https://wandb.ai/ai2-llm/olmo-small/reports/Training-Logs-of-OLMo2-0425-1B--VmlldzoxMjUzOTUxNA)
for detailed configurations

No intentional architectural modification to the upstream model is introduced.
pull_requests.experiments_summary:
The training loss decreased consistently throughout the training run.

No persistent loss spikes, non-finite gradients, or optimizer instability were observed. 
Gradient norm remained stable after the initial warmup period. 

The complete loss, learning-rate, gradient-norm, throughput, and step-time curves are available in the linked W&B report.

The OLMo2 implementation can be constructed, trained, checkpointed, and evaluated successfully in the current repository. 

The training run showed a stable decrease in training and validation loss. No persistent numerical instability was observed. 

All required benchmarks completed successfully.
pull_requests.conclusion:
This PR successfully adds OLMo2 as a functional root architecture in the repository. 

The implementation passes model-construction, forward, backward, checkpoint, smoke-training, W&B logging, and benchmark-pipeline validation. 

The architecture is suitable for use as a parent node in future architecture proposals. Full-scale training reproduction and upstream-checkpoint equivalence should be tracked as separate validation work.

merge_checklist.label: The linked proposal is approved.
The implementation matches the official architecture.
The upstream source and immutable revision are recorded.
Forward, backward, checkpoint, and training validation pass.
Training and evaluation configurations are committed.
Public W&B logs and benchmark results are linked.
Tests, documentation, and CI pass.
Required reviewers approve the PR.

files.excerpt:
# Copyright 2025 the HuggingFace Team. All rights reserved.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

from ...utils import _LazyModule
from ...utils.import_utils import define_import_structure


if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from .configuration_olmo3 import *
    from .modeling_olmo3 import *
else:
    import sys

    _file = globals()["__file__"]
    sys.modules[__name__] = _LazyModule(__name__, _file, define_import_structure(_file), module_spec=__spec__)

class Olmo3Config(PreTrainedConfig):
    r"""
    Example:

    ```python
    >>> from transformers import Olmo3Model, Olmo3Config

    >>> # Initializing a Olmo3 7B style configuration
    >>> configuration = Olmo3Config()

    >>> # Initializing a model from the Olmo3 7B style configuration
    >>> model = Olmo3Model(configuration)

    >>> # Accessing the model configuration
    >>> configuration = model.config
    ```
    """

    model_type = "olmo3"
    keys_to_ignore_at_inference = ["past_key_values"]
    base_model_tp_plan = {
        "layers.*.self_attn.q_proj": "colwise_gather_output",  # we need to replicate here due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.self_attn.k_proj": "colwise_gather_output",  # we need to replicate here due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.self_attn.v_proj": "colwise_gather_output",  # we need to replicate here due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.self_attn.o_proj": "rowwise_split_input",  # input is replicated due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.mlp.gate_proj": "colwise",
        "layers.*.mlp.up_proj": "colwise",
        "layers.*.mlp.down_proj": "rowwise",
    }
    base_model_pp_plan = {
        "embed_tokens": (["input_ids"], ["inputs_embeds"]),
        "layers": (["hidden_states", "attention_mask"], ["hidden_states"]),
        "norm": (["hidden_states"], ["hidden_states"]),
    }

    vocab_size: int = 50304
    hidden_size: int = 4096
    intermediate_size: int = 11008
    num_hidden_layers: int = 32
    num_attention_heads: int = 32
    num_key_value_heads: int | None = None
    hidden_act: str = "silu"
    max_position_embeddings: int = 2048
    initializer_range: float = 0.02
    use_cache: bool = True
    pad_token_id: int | None = 1
    bos_token_id: int | None = None
    eos_token_id: int | list[int] | None = 50279

class Olmo3RMSNorm(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, hidden_size, eps: float = 1e-6) -> None:
        """
        Olmo3RMSNorm is equivalent to T5LayerNorm
        """
        super().__init__()
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(hidden_size))
        self.variance_epsilon = eps

    def forward(self, hidden_states) -> torch.Tensor:
        input_dtype = hidden_states.dtype
        hidden_states = hidden_states.to(torch.float32)
        variance = hidden_states.pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True)
        hidden_states = hidden_states * torch.rsqrt(variance + self.variance_epsilon)
        return (self.weight * hidden_states).to(input_dtype)

    def extra_repr(self):
        return f"{tuple(self.weight.shape)}, eps={self.variance_epsilon}"


def repeat_kv(hidden_states: torch.Tensor, n_rep: int) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    This is the equivalent of torch.repeat_interleave(x, dim=1, repeats=n_rep). The hidden states go from (batch,
    num_key_value_heads, seqlen, head_dim) to (batch, num_attention_heads, seqlen, head_dim)
    """
    batch, num_key_value_heads, slen, head_dim = hidden_states.shape
    if n_rep == 1:
        return hidden_states
    hidden_states = hidden_states[:, :, None, :, :].expand(batch, num_key_value_heads, n_rep, slen, head_dim)
    return hidden_states.reshape(batch, num_key_value_heads * n_rep, slen, head_dim)


def eager_attention_forward(
    module: nn.Module,
    query: torch.Tensor,
    key: torch.Tensor,
    value: torch.Tensor,
    attention_mask: torch.Tensor | None,
    scaling: float,
    dropout: float = 0.0,
    **kwargs: Unpack[TransformersKwargs],
):
    key_states = repeat_kv(key, module.num_key_value_groups)
    value_states = repeat_kv(value, module.num_key_value_groups)

    attn_weights = torch.matmul(query, key_states.transpose(2, 3)) * scaling
    if attention_mask is not None:
        attn_weights = attn_weights + attention_mask

class Olmo3Config(Olmo2Config):
    r"""
    Example:

    ```python
    >>> from transformers import Olmo3Model, Olmo3Config

    >>> # Initializing a Olmo3 7B style configuration
    >>> configuration = Olmo3Config()

    >>> # Initializing a model from the Olmo3 7B style configuration
    >>> model = Olmo3Model(configuration)

    >>> # Accessing the model configuration
    >>> configuration = model.config
    ```
    """

    model_type = "olmo3"
    default_theta = 500000.0
    keys_to_ignore_at_inference = ["past_key_values"]
    base_model_tp_plan = {
        "layers.*.self_attn.q_proj": "colwise_gather_output",  # we need to replicate here due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.self_attn.k_proj": "colwise_gather_output",  # we need to replicate here due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.self_attn.v_proj": "colwise_gather_output",  # we need to replicate here due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.self_attn.o_proj": "rowwise_split_input",  # input is replicated due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.mlp.gate_proj": "colwise",
        "layers.*.mlp.up_proj": "colwise",
        "layers.*.mlp.down_proj": "rowwise",
    }
    base_model_pp_plan = {
        "embed_tokens": (["input_ids"], ["inputs_embeds"]),
        "layers": (["hidden_states", "attention_mask"], ["hidden_states"]),
        "norm": (["hidden_states"], ["hidden_states"]),
    }

    sliding_window: int | None = 4096
    layer_types: list[str] | None = None

    def __post_init__(self, **kwargs):
        if self.num_key_value_heads is None:
            self.num_key_value_heads = self.num_attention_heads

        if self.layer_types is None:
            self.layer_types = [
                "sliding_attention" if (i + 1) % 4 != 0 else "full_attention" for i in range(self.num_hidden_layers)
            ]

from typing import TYPE_CHECKING

from ...utils import _LazyModule
from ...utils.import_utils import define_import_structure


if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from .configuration_olmo3 import *
    from .modeling_olmo3 import *
else:
    import sys

    _file = globals()["__file__"]
    sys.modules[__name__] = _LazyModule(__name__, _file, define_import_structure(_file), module_spec=__spec__)

class Olmo3Config(PreTrainedConfig):
    r"""
    Example:

    ```python
    >>> from transformers import Olmo3Model, Olmo3Config

    >>> # Initializing a Olmo3 7B style configuration
    >>> configuration = Olmo3Config()

    >>> # Initializing a model from the Olmo3 7B style configuration
    >>> model = Olmo3Model(configuration)

    >>> # Accessing the model configuration
    >>> configuration = model.config
    ```
    """

    model_type = "olmo3"
    keys_to_ignore_at_inference = ["past_key_values"]
    base_model_tp_plan = {
        "layers.*.self_attn.q_proj": "colwise_gather_output",  # we need to replicate here due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.self_attn.k_proj": "colwise_gather_output",  # we need to replicate here due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.self_attn.v_proj": "colwise_gather_output",  # we need to replicate here due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.self_attn.o_proj": "rowwise_split_input",  # input is replicated due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.mlp.gate_proj": "colwise",
        "layers.*.mlp.up_proj": "colwise",
        "layers.*.mlp.down_proj": "rowwise",
    }
    base_model_pp_plan = {
        "embed_tokens": (["input_ids"], ["inputs_embeds"]),
        "layers": (["hidden_states", "attention_mask"], ["hidden_states"]),
        "norm": (["hidden_states"], ["hidden_states"]),
    }

    vocab_size: int = 50304
    hidden_size: int = 4096
    intermediate_size: int = 11008
    num_hidden_layers: int = 32
    num_attention_heads: int = 32
    num_key_value_heads: int | None = None
    hidden_act: str = "silu"
    max_position_embeddings: int = 2048
    initializer_range: float = 0.02
    use_cache: bool = True
    pad_token_id: int | None = 1
    bos_token_id: int | None = None
    eos_token_id: int | list[int] | None = 50279

class Olmo3RMSNorm(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, hidden_size, eps: float = 1e-6) -> None:
        """
        Olmo3RMSNorm is equivalent to T5LayerNorm
        """
        super().__init__()
        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(hidden_size))
        self.variance_epsilon = eps

    def forward(self, hidden_states) -> torch.Tensor:
        input_dtype = hidden_states.dtype
        hidden_states = hidden_states.to(torch.float32)
        variance = hidden_states.pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True)
        hidden_states = hidden_states * torch.rsqrt(variance + self.variance_epsilon)
        return (self.weight * hidden_states).to(input_dtype)

    def extra_repr(self):
        return f"{tuple(self.weight.shape)}, eps={self.variance_epsilon}"


def repeat_kv(hidden_states: torch.Tensor, n_rep: int) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    This is the equivalent of torch.repeat_interleave(x, dim=1, repeats=n_rep). The hidden states go from (batch,
    num_key_value_heads, seqlen, head_dim) to (batch, num_attention_heads, seqlen, head_dim)
    """
    batch, num_key_value_heads, slen, head_dim = hidden_states.shape
    if n_rep == 1:
        return hidden_states
    hidden_states = hidden_states[:, :, None, :, :].expand(batch, num_key_value_heads, n_rep, slen, head_dim)
    return hidden_states.reshape(batch, num_key_value_heads * n_rep, slen, head_dim)


def eager_attention_forward(
    module: nn.Module,
    query: torch.Tensor,
    key: torch.Tensor,
    value: torch.Tensor,
    attention_mask: torch.Tensor | None,
    scaling: float,
    dropout: float = 0.0,
    **kwargs: Unpack[TransformersKwargs],
):
    key_states = repeat_kv(key, module.num_key_value_groups)
    value_states = repeat_kv(value, module.num_key_value_groups)

    attn_weights = torch.matmul(query, key_states.transpose(2, 3)) * scaling
    if attention_mask is not None:
        attn_weights = attn_weights + attention_mask

class Olmo3Config(Olmo2Config):
    r"""
    Example:

    ```python
    >>> from transformers import Olmo3Model, Olmo3Config

    >>> # Initializing a Olmo3 7B style configuration
    >>> configuration = Olmo3Config()

    >>> # Initializing a model from the Olmo3 7B style configuration
    >>> model = Olmo3Model(configuration)

    >>> # Accessing the model configuration
    >>> configuration = model.config
    ```
    """

    model_type = "olmo3"
    default_theta = 500000.0
    keys_to_ignore_at_inference = ["past_key_values"]
    base_model_tp_plan = {
        "layers.*.self_attn.q_proj": "colwise_gather_output",  # we need to replicate here due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.self_attn.k_proj": "colwise_gather_output",  # we need to replicate here due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.self_attn.v_proj": "colwise_gather_output",  # we need to replicate here due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.self_attn.o_proj": "rowwise_split_input",  # input is replicated due to the added norm on q and k
        "layers.*.mlp.gate_proj": "colwise",
        "layers.*.mlp.up_proj": "colwise",
        "layers.*.mlp.down_proj": "rowwise",
    }
    base_model_pp_plan = {
        "embed_tokens": (["input_ids"], ["inputs_embeds"]),
        "layers": (["hidden_states", "attention_mask"], ["hidden_states"]),
        "norm": (["hidden_states"], ["hidden_states"]),
    }

    sliding_window: int | None = 4096
    layer_types: list[str] | None = None

    def __post_init__(self, **kwargs):
        if self.num_key_value_heads is None:
            self.num_key_value_heads = self.num_attention_heads

        if self.layer_types is None:
            self.layer_types = [
                "sliding_attention" if (i + 1) % 4 != 0 else "full_attention" for i in range(self.num_hidden_layers)
            ]
